content.problemStatement:
A linear chandelier is held up by NNN supports. The ithi^{th}ith support initially holds up a weight WiW_iWi‚Äã, and will collapse if the weight on it is **more than or equal to** AiA_iAi‚Äã. Note that initially, Wi<AiW_i \lt A_iWi‚Äã<Ai‚Äã for all 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N.

Whenever Wi‚â•AiW_i\ge A_iWi‚Äã‚â•Ai‚Äã for some support, the following happens:


The ithi^{th}ith support is destroyed;
Let xxx and yyy be uniformly random non-negative integers such that x+y=Wix+y=W_ix+y=Wi‚Äã.

Add xxx to Wi‚àí1W_{i-1}Wi‚àí1‚Äã and add yyy to Wi+1W_{i+1}Wi+1‚Äã.
Note that:


If either one of the neighbours do not exist, all the weight is added to the remaining neighbour.
If both neighbours do not exist, the weight dissipates.
For all 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N, find the **minimum** weight you need to add to WiW_iWi‚Äã, so that there is a nonzero chance all supports end up destroyed.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains NNN, the number of supports.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers W1,W2,‚Ä¶,WNW_1, W_2, \ldots, W_NW1‚Äã,W2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,WN‚Äã, denoting initial weight on  each support.
The third line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, denoting the minimum load at which a support destroys.


Output Format
For each test, output NNN space-separated integers. The ithi^{th}ith of these should be the **minimum weight** you need to add to the ithi^{th}ith chandelier, so that there is a nonzero chance that all supports end up destroyed.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The following is possible if we add weight 111 to support 222:

Support 222 collapses, since W2=2‚â•2W_2=2\ge 2W2‚Äã=2‚â•2. A weight x=1x=1x=1 gets added to W1W_1W1‚Äã, and a weight y=1y=1y=1 gets added to W3W_3W3‚Äã.
Support 111 collapses, since W1=2‚â•2W_1=2\ge 2W1‚Äã=2‚â•2.
Support 333 collapses, since W3=2‚â•2W_3=2\ge 2W3‚Äã=2‚â•2.

If we add zero weight, then nothing would happen and all supports would stay standing. So the answer for i=2i=2i=2 is 111.
Test case 222: We have to add 999999999999999999999999999 weight to the only support to make it collapse.
Test case 333: Note that we could add a weight of 111 to support 111 to make it collapse, but then, there would be no way to make the second support collapse. We can show that we have to add at least 444344434443 weight to support 111 to make it possible for both supports to end up destroyed.
